title: Voxtral / Speech Integration via dedicated voice-runtime service
goal: Integrate Voxtral and speech/audio capabilities for Android, laptops and local desktop setups through a dedicated voice-runtime service instead of hard-coding model/runtime logic into each client.

done_scope: Architecture option C is defined and aligned with hub-worker boundaries.
Core API and environment contract outlines exist.
Hub voice routes and provider catalog capability wiring are implemented.
Voxtral adapter, normalization, fallback chain, and device detection are implemented.
Compose overlay and voice runtime packaging are integrated.
Android/Desktop/CLI voice access paths now use Hub-facing APIs.
Voice exposure policy and explicit goal-approval boundary are enforced.
missing_scope: Privacy hardening for stored audio policy is still pending.
CI/release verification coverage for voice flows is still pending.
User-facing release/quickstart docs for final rollout are still pending.

architecture_guardrails: Hub remains control plane: routing, policy, approvals, and audit ownership stay in Hub.
voice-runtime is execution worker only; no worker-to-worker orchestration.
Dangerous actions from voice never bypass existing approval mechanisms.

core_decision.name: Dedicated voice-runtime service
core_decision.summary: Create a separate local voice service exposing stable Ananta-facing audio APIs. Under the hood it may use Voxtral, whisper.cpp, faster-whisper, llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio, or future multimodal runtimes.
reasoning: Keeps Android, laptop UI, CLI and future clients thin.
Avoids binding Ananta to Ollama or LM Studio-specific audio behavior.
Allows fallback chains and hardware-specific optimization without changing client code.
Fits Ananta's hub-worker and provider-adapter architecture.
Makes security, audit, permissions and capability exposure easier to control centrally.

high_level_flow: Client sends audio to Ananta Hub or directly to voice-runtime only when explicitly allowed.
Ananta Hub validates auth, policy, size limits and requested capability.
Ananta Hub forwards audio job to voice-runtime.
voice-runtime selects Voxtral or fallback backend.
voice-runtime returns transcript, command intent, structured result or multimodal response.
Ananta Hub stores audit metadata and dispatches resulting task/goal/action according to policy.

responsibilities: Apply policy, rate limits, size limits and audit logging.
Map transcripts or voice commands into Ananta goals/tasks.
Expose health, model catalog and capability endpoints.
Implement fallback chain and hardware-specific backend selection.
Send audio to Ananta voice endpoint.
Avoid direct runtime coupling where possible.
Use the same Ananta API as Android.
Avoid direct dependency on Voxtral, Ollama or LM Studio.

runtime_modes.description: Runs voice-runtime locally on CPU with smaller/faster fallback models.
Runs voice-runtime locally with GPU acceleration.
Runs Ananta plus voice-runtime through Docker Compose for reproducible development.
Ananta talks to an already-running voice-runtime on the host or another LAN machine.

required_new.name: VOICE_PROVIDER
required_new.description: Logical provider name used by Ananta for voice/audio capabilities.

required_new.name: VOICE_RUNTIME_URL
required_new.description: Base URL for the dedicated voice-runtime service.

required_new.name: VOICE_FALLBACK_MODEL
required_new.description: Fallback transcription model if Voxtral is unavailable or too slow.

required_new.name: VOICE_MAX_AUDIO_MB
required_new.description: Maximum accepted audio upload size in MiB.

required_new.name: VOICE_TIMEOUT_SEC
required_new.description: Maximum processing time for a voice job.

required_new.name: VOICE_DIRECT_CLIENT_ACCESS
required_new.description: Whether clients may call voice-runtime directly. Default false; route through Ananta Hub.

optional_new.name: VOICE_RUNTIME_MODEL_PATH
optional_new.description: Optional local model path or mounted model directory.

optional_new.name: VOICE_ENABLE_STREAMING
optional_new.description: Enable streaming partial transcription once implemented.

optional_new.name: VOICE_STORE_AUDIO
optional_new.description: Whether raw audio may be stored for debugging. Default false for privacy.

ananta_hub_public_api.body: multipart/form-data with audio file plus optional language, prompt, model, mode
multipart/form-data with audio file plus optional command_context
audio file, optional create_tasks boolean, optional governance mode

response_200.text: Bitte erstelle ein neues Task fuer die Eclipse Plugin Installation.

response_200.transcript: Bitte analysiere todo.voxtral.json und schlage Verbesserungen vor.
Implementiere Voice Runtime MVP mit Mock Backend.

error.message: Voice goal creation is not allowed for current user/policy mode.

tasks.title: Document voice-runtime architecture decision
tasks.description: Create a short architecture note explaining why voice-runtime is separate from Android, Ollama and LM Studio.
acceptance: Architecture note contains chosen option C.
Clearly separates Ananta Hub API from runtime internals.
Mentions privacy, policy and fallback reasons.

tasks.title: Define capability names
tasks.description: Add stable capability names for audio_input, transcription, voice_command and multimodal_audio_prompt.
acceptance: Provider catalog can expose voice capabilities.
No direct client assumption about Voxtral exists.

tasks.title: Define public Ananta voice API contract
tasks.description: Specify request and response schemas for transcription, voice command and voice goal creation.

tasks.title: Create voice-runtime package skeleton
tasks.description: Add a small Python service package for the dedicated voice-runtime.

tasks.title: Implement mock transcription backend
tasks.description: Add a deterministic mock backend for tests and CI without real model downloads.
acceptance: CI can test voice API without GPU/model.
Mock backend can simulate timeout and backend unavailable errors.

tasks.title: Implement /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint
tasks.description: Accept multipart audio upload and return transcript using selected backend.
acceptance: Accepts wav, mp3, m4a and webm where available.

tasks.title: Add model catalog endpoint
tasks.description: Expose available voice models and capabilities from voice-runtime.
acceptance: GET /v1/models returns primary and fallback models.
Each model declares capabilities and device preference.
Health endpoint shows selected backend and device.

tasks.title: Add VoiceProvider adapter in Ananta backend
tasks.description: Create an adapter that calls voice-runtime and hides backend-specific implementation details.
acceptance: Adapter reads VOICE_RUNTIME_URL and timeout config.
Adapter supports transcribe and voice_command calls.
Adapter maps runtime errors to Ananta errors.

tasks.title: Expose Ananta Hub voice routes
tasks.description: Add authenticated Hub endpoints for transcription, command and goal creation.
acceptance: POST /v1/voice/command returns transcript and proposed intent.
POST /v1/voice/goal can create a goal only through existing policy path.

tasks.title: Register voice capabilities in provider catalog
tasks.description: Extend existing provider catalog/capability response to include local_voice when available.
acceptance: Unavailable runtime reports degraded/unavailable, not hard failure.
Frontend can detect whether voice UI should be shown.

tasks.title: Add audit metadata for voice actions
tasks.description: Store transcript metadata and action intent without storing raw audio by default.
acceptance: Raw audio is not stored unless VOICE_STORE_AUDIO=true.
Audit includes user, endpoint, model, duration and created goal/task id.

tasks.title: Implement Voxtral backend adapter
tasks.description: Add backend implementation for Voxtral in voice-runtime. Keep model loading isolated from API routes.
acceptance: Backend supports explicit model path or model id.

tasks.title: Add audio normalization pipeline
tasks.description: Normalize incoming audio to the format expected by Voxtral/fallback backends.
acceptance: Rejects unsupported formats with useful errors.
Keeps temporary files scoped and deleted after request.

tasks.title: Implement backend fallback chain
tasks.description: If Voxtral is unavailable, too slow or unsupported on current hardware, fallback to configured transcription backend.
acceptance: Response includes warning when fallback was used.

tasks.title: Add hardware/device detection
tasks.description: Detect cpu/cuda/vulkan/metal where practical and select the best supported backend.

tasks.title: Add Dockerfile for voice-runtime
tasks.description: Create a dedicated Dockerfile with mock/fallback backend support by default and optional Voxtral dependencies documented.
acceptance: Image builds without downloading huge models by default.

tasks.title: Add docker-compose voice overlay
tasks.description: Create compose overlay to run voice-runtime next to Ananta Hub/Worker.
acceptance: Host access mode documented for LM Studio/Ollama-style host runtimes.

tasks.title: Extend setup templates
tasks.description: Add voice-related environment variables to env templates without enabling raw audio storage by default.

tasks.title: Add laptop profile docs
tasks.description: Document recommended setup for CPU laptop, GPU laptop, WSL2/Linux and external GPU host.
acceptance: Includes LM Studio/Ollama as optional text backends, not required voice layer.
Includes direct host runtime URL examples.
Includes privacy and local network warning.

tasks.title: Refactor Android Voxtral integration to use Ananta voice API
tasks.description: Move Android-specific Voxtral logic behind the shared Hub voice endpoint.
acceptance: Android no longer needs to know exact Voxtral runtime details.
Android sends audio to Hub voice endpoint.
Existing Android voice feature still works.

tasks.title: Add desktop microphone flow
tasks.description: Allow browser/laptop UI to record microphone input and send to Ananta Hub.
acceptance: Voice button appears only when capability is available.
User grants browser microphone permission explicitly.
Transcript is shown before creating risky goals/actions.

tasks.title: Add CLI voice file command
tasks.description: Allow CLI users to submit an existing audio file for transcription or goal creation.
acceptance: ananta voice goal <file> asks for review before creating tasks unless explicitly allowed.

tasks.title: Add voice exposure policy
tasks.description: Ensure voice endpoints obey existing exposure policy concepts and are not accidentally exposed remotely.
acceptance: Direct voice-runtime access is disabled by default.

tasks.title: Add approval boundary for voice-created actions
tasks.description: Voice commands must not silently execute dangerous tasks. They should propose intent/goal and route through existing approval paths.
acceptance: Voice-created shell/tool actions require approval under safe/strict modes.

tasks.title: Add privacy defaults
tasks.description: Default to no raw audio persistence and document the implications of enabling storage.
acceptance: Logs do not contain raw audio or excessive transcript content unless debug mode is explicit.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for voice-runtime mock backend
tasks.description: Test health, model catalog, transcription success and error handling.

tasks.title: Add Ananta Hub contract tests for voice routes
tasks.description: Ensure public voice API response shapes remain stable.

tasks.title: Add Docker smoke test
tasks.description: Start compose stack with voice-runtime mock backend and verify Hub capability endpoint.

tasks.title: Add optional live Voxtral test flag
tasks.description: Live Voxtral tests must be opt-in because models/hardware are heavy.
acceptance: Default CI skips live model test.

tasks.title: Write user-facing voice quickstart
tasks.description: Document how to start Ananta with voice-runtime on a normal laptop.
acceptance: Includes troubleshooting for microphone, Docker networking and missing models.

tasks.title: Add release checklist entries
tasks.description: Extend release checklist with voice-specific security, privacy and runtime checks.
acceptance: Checklist includes no raw audio by default.
Checklist includes auth and exposure policy checks.

tasks.title: Document known limitations
tasks.description: Document expected limitations around hardware, latency, model size and language quality.
acceptance: No claim that all laptops can run Voxtral well.

tasks.title: Define public Ananta voice API contract
Add VoiceProvider adapter in Ananta backend
Register voice capabilities in provider catalog
Add audit metadata for voice actions
Refactor Android Voxtral integration to use Ananta voice API
Add approval boundary for voice-created actions
Add unit tests for voice-runtime mock backend
Add Ananta Hub contract tests for voice routes
Add optional live Voxtral test flag

P0: Architecture decision note committed and reviewed.
Capability names published in provider contracts.
Public voice API contract documented including auth, limits, and error shape.
P1: Mock backend supports success + timeout/unavailable simulation.
/v1/audio/transcriptions enforces file-size and returns stable error JSON.
P2: Hub routes /v1/voice/transcribe, /command, /goal are wired through adapter.
Provider catalog reports voice capability availability/degraded status.
Voice route contract tests pass with mock runtime.
P3: Voxtral adapter loads model with explicit error reporting.
Audio normalization handles targeted input formats and cleanup.
Fallback chain works and exposes warning when fallback backend is used.
P4: Docker image builds without default heavy model download.
Compose overlay starts Hub + voice-runtime integration path.
Environment templates include safe defaults and preserve non-voice setup.
P5: Android, desktop, and CLI use Hub voice API instead of runtime-specific coupling.
Transcript preview appears before risky action/goal execution.
P6: Auth and exposure policy enforced on all voice routes.
Risky voice-created actions require explicit approval.
Raw audio persistence remains opt-in and disabled by default.
P7: Unit + contract tests pass in CI without GPU/model downloads.
Docker smoke test verifies health and capability endpoints.
Optional live Voxtral test remains opt-in and clearly documented.
P8: Quickstart and limitation docs are published and accurate.
Release evidence references voice runtime readiness and degraded behavior.

definition_of_done: Ananta Hub exposes authenticated voice endpoints.
voice-runtime can run with mock backend in CI.
voice-runtime can run with Voxtral or configured fallback locally.
Android uses shared Ananta voice API instead of direct hard-coded runtime coupling.
Laptop/browser/CLI clients can use the same API.
Provider catalog exposes voice capability status.
Raw audio is not stored by default.
Voice-created actions go through existing policy and approval boundaries.
Docker overlay and quickstart docs exist.
Contract tests and smoke tests cover unavailable runtime, oversized upload and successful mock transcription.

risks.risk: Voxtral may be too heavy for some laptops.
risks.mitigation: Keep fallback backend mandatory and document CPU-only profile.

risks.risk: Ollama/LM Studio audio behavior may differ or be incomplete.
risks.mitigation: Treat them as optional backends, not the core abstraction.

risks.risk: Voice commands can be misheard and trigger wrong actions.
risks.mitigation: Default to transcript review and approval before risky actions.

risks.risk: Raw audio can contain sensitive data.
risks.mitigation: Do not store raw audio by default; document explicit opt-in.

risks.risk: Docker networking differs between Windows, WSL2, Linux and macOS.
risks.mitigation: Document host.docker.internal, compose DNS and direct host mode separately.

recommended_first_milestone.name: MVP voice-runtime mock + Hub-safe voice command route
recommended_first_milestone.success_signal: A user can send an audio file to Ananta Hub, receive a transcript from mock voice-runtime, and create only approval-routed voice intents while capability status is exposed in provider catalog.

recommended_execution_order: 1) P0 contracts first, then P1 mock runtime, then P2 Hub integration.
2) Land P6 security/approval boundaries before enabling client UX paths in P5.
3) Keep P3 Voxtral adapter optional until mock+fallback path is stable in CI.
4) Treat P7 contract tests as a merge gate for any voice API change.
5) Run P8 docs and release checklist before claiming production readiness.
quality_gates: No endpoint merges without auth and policy checks.
Every new route has contract tests and degraded-path tests.
Fallback backend behavior must be explicit in response warnings.
notes: Keep this file as planning source, not runtime config.
Prefer small, reviewable PRs per phase.
Do not make raw audio storage the default.
Do not let voice commands bypass existing Ananta approval and policy mechanisms.
Keep Voxtral backend replaceable so future audio models can be added without client changes.
